content.full_text:
RUDNANSKY, THOMSEN & VARNER
AN ASSOCIATION OF ATTORNEYS

RICHARD B. RUDNANSKY
LESLIE A. THOMSEN
BRYAN J. VARNER

900 COLLEGE AVENUE
SUITE B
SANTA ROSA, CALIFORNIA 95404

TELEPHONE (707) 578-3303
FAX (707) 542-2356

October 7, 1992


Ms. Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Breeze:

     Please be advised that this is a request for a
formal opinion pertaining to the potential appointment
of Petaluma City Councilmember Bonnie Nelson to the
Burbank Housing Development Corporation.

     Burbank Housing Development Corporation
(hereinafter referred to as "BHDC"), a California non-
profit public benefit corporation was formed in 1980 by
action of the Sonoma County Board of Supervisors.
BHDC's Board of Directors consist of eleven members:
the Executive Director of the Sonoma County Housing
Authority, the Auditor-Controller and County
Administrator of the County of Sonoma and their
designees, one director appointed by each of the five
members of the Board of Supervisors, and the three members
at large selected by the Board of Directors.

     BHDC's mission was to implement portions of
the Housing Element of the 1978 Sonoma County General
Plan through joint housing construction and
rehabilitation ventures with the Sonoma County Housing
Authority and other public agencies including the City
of Petaluma. BHDC's responsibilities in these areas
continue through the 1989 General Plan. BHDC's Articles
of Incorporation direct to engage in activities designed
to increase housing and economic opportunities for low
and moderate income persons; and to engage in community
development projects.

     Documents generated at the time of the BHDC's
incorporation show that BHDC was to operate as a private
sector counterpart to the Sonoma County Housing

Ms. Breeze
October 7, 1992
Page 2

Authority and the Santa Rosa Housing Authority. The
primary reasons for forming a private entity were to
provide more flexibility, avoid the negative label of
"public housing" and to act as a focus of charitable
gifts for programs for low and moderate priced housing.
Since its incorporation, BHDC has been almost
exclusively engaged in activities that could be
conducted by a public housing authority and which are
financed by public grants and loans. With few
exceptions, BHDC is the functional equivalent of a
public housing authority.

Councilmember Bonnie Nelson, elected to the
City Council in November, 1989, and began service in
January, 1990, is currently a tenant in one of the
affordable housing projects developed by BHDC within the
city limits of Petaluma.

Said project is a 24-unit rental development,
Madrone Village, which is owned by a limited membership
(because it was financed with tax credit investments) in
which Burbank has only a one percent interest, albeit as
managing general partner, and the tax credit investors
own the other 99 percent.

Income eligibility for tenants, rents and all
other terms of tenancy are strictly regulated pursuant
to Title 42 of the Internal Revenue Code. Burbank, even
as managing general partner has virtually no discretion
in dealing with tenants. Indeed, Burbank is further
removed from active property management and tenant
relations, by its employment of a professional
management company to deal with the tenants in all
landlord-tenant matters.

Additionally, except for its responsibilities
as managing general partner, Burbank could formally
relinquish to the property manager any discretion it
might have in dealing with Ms. Nelson as tenant; and,
conversely Ms. Nelson would abstain in the Burbank Board
and the City Council on any matters which might come up
concerning the development in which she lives, should
any such arise, which is highly unlikely at this point.

Finally, it should be noted that Ms. Nelson's
tenancy at Madrone Village predates both her election to
the Council and her possible appointment to the Burbank
Board.

A member of the Board of Supervisors for the
County of Sonoma desires to appoint Ms. Nelson as a

Ms. Breeze
October 7, 1992
Page 3

member of the Board of Directors of BHDC and, therefore,
if this is accomplished, she will be holding that office
as well as the office of Councilmember of the City of
Petaluma. She would not receive any compensation or
reimbursement from Burbank.

From time to time, BHDC appears before the
City Council seeking environmental and zoning approvals
for affordable housing projects within the City of
Petaluma, requesting and receiving in lieu housing fees
and other considerations (i.e., waiver of application
fees, etc.) to assist BHDC in the development and
construction of affordable housing both rental and owned
units. In many instances, BHDC and the City enter into
agreements or contracts relating to affordability
restrictions with respect to tenants and purchasers of
BHDC units.

It is anticipated that such appearances before
the City and activities within the City will continue to
come before the Petaluma City Council.

Therefore, the question has become if
Ms. Nelson is appointed to the Board of Directors of
BHDC, would her presence on the Board of BHDC and the
Petaluma City Council create a conflict of interest
and/or invalidate any decisions of the City of Petaluma
pertaining to a BHDC project within the City limits
assuming that the only connection that Ms. Nelson has
with BHDC, other than as a member of the Board of
Directors or a Councilmember for the City of Petaluma,
is her tenancy arrangement? What disclosure would be
required, if any, by Ms. Nelson as a member of the
Board of Directors of BHDC and as a member of the City
Council of the City of Petaluma?

Thank you for your anticipated courtesy and
consideration.

Very truly yours,

RICHARD R. RUDNANSKY
Petaluma City Attorney

RRR:taz

cc: Ms. Bonnie Nelson

sections.question: Therefore, the question has become if Ms. Nelson is appointed to the Board of Directors of BHDC, would her presence on the Board of BHDC and the Petaluma City Council create a conflict of interest and/or invalidate any decisions of the City of Petaluma pertaining to a BHDC project within the City limits assuming that the only connection that Ms. Nelson has with BHDC, other than as a member of the Board of Directors or a Councilmember for the City of Petaluma, is her tenancy arrangement? What disclosure would be required, if any, by Ms. Nelson as a member of the Board of Directors of BHDC and as a member of the City Council of the City of Petaluma?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the appointment of Petaluma City Councilmember Bonnie Nelson to the Board of Directors of Burbank Housing Development Corporation would create a conflict of interest, invalidate City Council decisions regarding BHDC projects, and what disclosure obligations would apply to her dual positions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/advice section present. The question section is clearly articulated near the end of the letter. Document provides detailed background about BHDC, Ms. Nelson's tenancy at a BHDC project, and her dual roles.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Therefore, the question has become if Ms. Nelson is appointed to the Board of Directors of BHDC, would her presence on the Board of BHDC and the Petaluma City Council create a conflict of interest and/or invalidate any decisions of the City of Petaluma pertaining to a BHDC project within the City limits assuming that the only connection that Ms. Nelson has with BHDC, other than as a member of the Board of Directors or a Councilmember for the City of Petaluma, is her tenancy arrangement? What disclosure would be required, if any, by Ms. Nelson as a member of the Board of Directors of BHDC and as a member of the City Council of the City of Petaluma?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney representing Petaluma City Councilmember Bonnie Nelson, seeking a formal FPPC opinion on whether her potential appointment to the Board of Directors of Burbank Housing Development Corporation would create a conflict of interest with her City Council position, and what disclosure requirements would apply.
